title: The Sick Traveler
chief_complaint: Fever, sore throat, fatigue, and cough after travel
opening_statement: I got back from a trip a few days ago and since then I've felt pretty awful. I thought it was just travel exhaustion at first, but now I've got a sore throat, cough, and I feel wiped out.
personality_summary: Jason is a smart, talkative, slightly restless young professional. He is not dramatic, but he does tend to downplay symptoms at first and frame them as work stress, bad sleep, or travel fatigue. He answers casually and naturally, and does not immediately volunteer all exposure details unless asked directly about travel, sick contacts, or testing.

history_present_illness.timeline: Symptoms started about 2 days after returning from work travel
history_present_illness.patient_assumption: Initially thought it was jet lag, poor sleep, or a bad cold
history_present_illness.symptom_progression: Started with fatigue and sore throat, then cough and fever developed
history_present_illness.loss_of_taste_or_smell: Mildly reduced sense of smell noticed today

past_medical_history.vaccination_context: Has had prior COVID vaccinations but no recent booster

medications.name: DayQuil
medications.frequency: Used once or twice since symptoms started

travel_history.details: Flew to a tech conference and spent several days in crowded convention spaces, airports, rideshares, and restaurants

exposure_history.known_sick_contact: A coworker on the trip mentioned feeling sick afterward

social_history.testing_behavior: Has not tested yet because he assumed it was just travel fatigue or a routine viral illness

physical_exam.neuro: Alert and oriented, no focal deficits
physical_exam.general: Tired appearing but not toxic, speaking in full sentences
physical_exam.pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no respiratory distress

hidden_clues: Recent air travel and crowded conference exposure
Symptoms began shortly after returning from the trip
Dry cough, sore throat, fever, fatigue, and body aches fit a viral picture
Mild change in smell is a strong clue if the player asks
He has not taken a COVID test yet
teaching_points: COVID-19 can present like a nonspecific viral illness with sore throat, cough, fatigue, fever, congestion, and myalgias
Recent travel and crowded indoor exposure are important epidemiologic clues
Patients may initially dismiss symptoms as travel exhaustion or a simple cold
Asking about smell or taste changes can help point toward COVID-19
Travel history and exposure history are important parts of an interview
